note: Köppen-collapsed climate regions for regionalizing phenology. iNaturalist month histograms are pulled per state (its queryable unit) then summed into these groups; each state is assigned its DOMINANT Köppen class where most iNaturalist observations concentrate (populated areas). Split states (WA/OR interior is arid but observations cluster on the wet coast; TX spans subtropical→arid but its metro corridor is subtropical; KS/NE/OK straddle the 100th-meridian transition) are assigned by observation-weighted dominance, not by land area. The app maps map-center → containing state → group, falling back to `national` (place_id=1) for the unknown region or a sparse species×region cell. This is a v1 binning; sub-state bounding-box refinement of split states is a documented future step.

continental.label: Humid continental
continental.summary: cold, snowy winters and warm–hot summers

subtropical.label: Humid subtropical
subtropical.summary: hot, humid summers and mild winters

semiarid.label: Semi-arid & mountain
semiarid.summary: dry interior west, deserts, high plains, and Rockies
